fields.narrative_intro: *A smug person and an exhausted person discuss AI speeding up software development in a Silicon Valley ping-pong room at 2am.*
fields.panel1:
[setup]
SMUG PERSON: "AI crushed it — coding phase down from 70 days to three."
fields.panel2:
[build]
EXHAUSTED PERSON: "Three days. Wow. What about requirements scoping?"
SMUG PERSON: "...also changed."
fields.panel3:
[pivot]
EXHAUSTED PERSON: "Changed how?"
SMUG PERSON: "Five days to forty. But the coding part is very fast."
fields.panel4:
[silent reaction]
[Visual: The exhausted person holds a Gantt chart that accordion-unfolds to the floor, stretching past the ping-pong table. The smug person's grin flickers exactly once.]
fields.panel5:
[punch]
EXHAUSTED PERSON: "So AI just gave devs the docs they've begged for since forever?"
SMUG PERSON: "We're calling it a breakthrough."
fields.panel6:
[button]
[Visual: A dog-eared comic-book copy of 'The Goal' sits on the ping-pong table. Someone has scrawled 'THE BOTTLENECK WAS NEVER THE CODE' on a sticky note attached to the cover.]

narrative_summary: A smug tech bro brags that AI cut coding from 70 days to 3, only for an exhausted colleague to unfurl a Gantt chart revealing requirements scoping ballooned from 5 to 40 days — exposing that AI just accidentally fulfilled developers' oldest wish: actually getting decent documentation.
rationale: Candidate 2 edges out Candidate 5 on punchline delivery: 'We're calling it a breakthrough' is a sharper, more satirically loaded button than the sticky-note gag, and the accordion-unfolding Gantt chart visual is a stronger earned sight gag than a cold coffee stare. Both tie on total but Candidate 2's panel 5 punch lands with more institutional absurdity rather than just restating the irony.
story_title: I don't think AI will make your processes go faster
place: in a Silicon Valley ping-pong room at 2am
hat_pair: firefighter helmet with a tiny rubber chicken mounted on top
crash helmet with a tiny working traffic light mounted on top
